proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +31.7pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio currently shows 100% Choppy regime concentration; if regime rotates to Bear, this boost will activate against an already concentrated book — monitor at regime transition
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (D-8 review) which may overstate edge if exit policy changes
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +31.7pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +31.7pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with zero Bear exposure, so activation is deferred and existing UP_TRI/Choppy concentration is not immediately compounded. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-03) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +25.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio regime concentration is at 100% Choppy with concentration flag triggered; any additional UP_TRI Metal entry would compound regime concentration if it fires in current regime
R-multiple 0.72 reflects D-8 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7; underlying R could be higher with exit fix, but TAKE_SMALL sizing is appropriate until resolved
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies the existing UP_TRI exposure (5 of 20 open) in a Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-03) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type×regime×sector = DOWN_TRI × Choppy × Auto (30.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type×regime×sector = DOWN_TRI × Choppy × Auto; recommended action: Pause new entries in sector=Auto AND signal_type=DOWN_TRI until Auto cell share drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits. Consider hedging the Auto short cluster with a long Auto proxy (e.g., index or sector ETF) to neutralize idiosyncratic Auto rebound risk.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If regime flips from Choppy to Bull or a sector-specific Auto rally triggers (earnings, policy), 6 concurrent Auto shorts unwind together — correlated stop-out cascade with no validated cohort edge to justify the loss. The 100% Choppy book also has zero diversification against a regime-shift gap.

evidence: DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto cell at 0.30 share (6 of 20 positions), exceeds 0.20 cell threshold
Matched cohort regime=Choppy__signal_type=DOWN_TRI is Candidate tier with n=3 — unvalidated edge
DOWN_TRI dominates book at 14/20 = 70% with all positions in single regime
Two additional cells (IT, Other) at 0.10 share each compound DOWN_TRI×Choppy exposure
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in sector=Auto AND signal_type=DOWN_TRI until Auto cell share drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits. Consider hedging the Auto short cluster with a long Auto proxy (e.g., index or sector ETF) to neutralize idiosyncratic Auto rebound risk.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If regime flips from Choppy to Bull or a sector-specific Auto rally triggers (earnings, policy), 6 concurrent Auto shorts unwind together — correlated stop-out cascade with no validated cohort edge to justify the loss. The 100% Choppy book also has zero diversification against a regime-shift gap.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (above 80% threshold) and the DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto cell sits at 30% share — well above the 20% cell threshold. The matched cohort is only Candidate tier (n=3), so this is unvalidated concentration: a single-sector short cluster representing nearly a third of the book with no statistical edge support.
